purpose: Design and implement a real operator-grade terminal UI for Ananta with modal navigation, section-local data loading, markdown rendering, and diagram preview support while preserving the hub-worker architecture.
goal: Provide a true terminal-native operator surface for Ananta that feels closer to vim, lazygit, and helix than a text dump, with actionable navigation, resilient partial rendering, markdown views, and Mermaid/PlantUML preview paths.
non_goals: No worker-to-worker orchestration through the TUI.
No direct mutation path that bypasses hub approval, policy, audit, or routing.
No big-bang replacement of the existing TUI shell before an additive migration path exists.
No requirement that rich diagram rendering must work in every terminal emulator.
architecture_guardrails: The hub remains the central control plane and owner of task orchestration.
The operator TUI is a client surface only and must consume hub-owned APIs or read-models.
Worker execution stays delegated through the existing hub task system.
New TUI actions must use explicit backend contracts rather than hidden local side effects.
Section rendering must degrade gracefully when backend sub-reads are slow or unavailable.

tasks.title: Operator TUI ADR and target architecture define
acceptance_criteria: A design record describes the new operator TUI as an additive client surface rather than a hub replacement.
The design explicitly preserves the hub-worker control model and names which responsibilities stay in the hub.
The design defines package boundaries for app shell, data adapters, renderers, and action dispatch.

tasks.title: Framework choice and runtime constraints evaluate
acceptance_criteria: A decision compares at least Textual and one simpler fallback approach against Ananta needs.
The decision covers async refresh, keyboard model, terminal compatibility, testing surface, and packaging impact.
The chosen framework is justified with concrete tradeoffs rather than style preference.

tasks.title: Mode model define for normal command inspect and edit flows
acceptance_criteria: A mode map exists for normal, command, inspect, and edit behavior.
Each mode documents allowed actions, escape paths, and input ownership.
Mode switching rules avoid ambiguous key handling for destructive actions.

tasks.title: Section taxonomy and navigation map define
acceptance_criteria: A navigation taxonomy identifies core sections such as Dashboard, Goals, Tasks, Artifacts, Knowledge, Config, System, and Audit.
Each section declares its primary data dependencies and fallback behavior.
The map distinguishes first-class terminal sections from browser-fallback or deferred sections.

tasks.title: Keyboard contract define
acceptance_criteria: A consistent keybinding contract exists for navigation, focus switching, search, command input, refresh, and back operations.
The contract includes vim-like defaults such as j/k, h/l, gg/G, slash search, and colon commands where appropriate.
The contract reserves explicit confirmation flows for risky actions.

tasks.title: Legacy shell migration strategy define
acceptance_criteria: The existing text-shell entrypoint remains available during migration.
A phased plan exists for introducing a new operator TUI package without breaking existing users.
The migration plan defines how old and new TUI entrypoints are selected and documented.

tasks.title: Operator TUI package scaffold create
acceptance_criteria: A new package exists for the real operator TUI rather than overloading the legacy shell in place.
The package layout separates shell, widgets/views, adapters, commands, and renderers.
The package is included in project packaging and importable from the editable install.

tasks.title: Root app shell with multi-pane layout implement
acceptance_criteria: The operator TUI provides a stable multi-pane shell with navigation, main content, and detail or status regions.
Layout resizing does not corrupt focus or cause content overlap across common terminal sizes.
The shell renders immediately before optional background section loads complete.

tasks.title: Focus management and pane switching implement
acceptance_criteria: Focus can move predictably between navigation, content, and detail panes from the keyboard only.
Focus state remains visible and testable.
Focus recovery works after refreshes, modal opens, and degraded backend responses.

tasks.title: Command palette or colon command line implement
acceptance_criteria: A command entry surface exists for actions such as refresh, section jump, task open, and goal create.
Unknown or blocked commands fail with explicit feedback rather than silent no-op behavior.
Command execution remains routed through typed client actions or read-model adapters.

tasks.title: Global status line implement
acceptance_criteria: The shell shows a compact global status line with endpoint, auth state, refresh state, and current mode.
Degraded or stale backend state is visible without flooding the screen.
Status updates do not require full app rerender.

tasks.title: In-app help and keybinding overlay implement
acceptance_criteria: A help surface lists active keybindings for the current mode and section.
The help view can be opened and closed without losing current selection context.
Help content reflects actual bound commands rather than static stale text.

tasks.title: Theme and density baseline define
acceptance_criteria: The operator TUI has a coherent visual baseline suitable for dense operational usage.
Highlighting differentiates selection, focus, warnings, blocked state, and destructive intent.
The theme remains readable in common dark terminal environments.

tasks.title: Section-local data adapter abstraction implement
acceptance_criteria: Each section can declare and load only the data it actually needs.
The abstraction avoids one giant fetch pass for every view render.
Adapter interfaces are small enough that sections do not depend on unrelated methods.

tasks.title: Incremental rendering and partial state model implement
acceptance_criteria: Visible panes render partial content as soon as required data is available.
Slow sub-panels can remain loading or degraded without blocking the entire screen.
The state model distinguishes loading, healthy, empty, degraded, and unauthorized states.

tasks.title: Per-section timeout and retry policy add
acceptance_criteria: Sections define their own timeout budgets rather than inheriting one global blocking timeout.
Timeout and retry behavior is observable and tunable.
A timed-out panel degrades locally instead of freezing navigation.

tasks.title: Background refresh scheduler add
acceptance_criteria: The operator TUI can refresh active views in the background without resetting selection state.
Background refresh avoids unbounded parallel request storms.
Users can force manual refresh when needed.

tasks.title: Degraded-state presentation model harden
acceptance_criteria: Backend_unreachable, auth_failed, timeout, policy_denied, and parse_error states are visually distinct.
A degraded panel still shows enough context to explain what failed.
Degraded handling is reused across sections instead of copied per view.

tasks.title: Authenticated profile bootstrap harden
acceptance_criteria: The operator TUI can bootstrap session auth from explicit token or ANANTA_USER and ANANTA_PASSWORD.
Auth bootstrap failures return actionable feedback rather than indefinite hangs.
Token reuse and refresh behavior are documented and covered by tests.

tasks.title: Markdown document renderer implement
acceptance_criteria: Markdown content can render headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, code blocks, and quotes in terminal form.
Long documents support scrolling without losing selection context elsewhere in the app.
Markdown rendering handles malformed but common content without crashing the app.

tasks.title: Artifact markdown view integrate
acceptance_criteria: Artifacts that are markdown or text-forward can open in a dedicated viewer pane.
The artifact viewer preserves metadata such as title, source task, and updated time.
Large artifacts degrade gracefully with pagination or preview limits.

tasks.title: Mermaid source detection and preview pipeline add
acceptance_criteria: The operator TUI can detect Mermaid blocks inside markdown or standalone artifacts.
Detected Mermaid content can be routed to a preview pipeline rather than shown only as raw text.
Preview failure falls back to source view with explicit explanation.

tasks.title: PlantUML source detection and preview pipeline add
acceptance_criteria: The operator TUI can detect PlantUML blocks or standalone documents.
The preview path supports at least one render mode and one reliable fallback mode.
The implementation names security and execution constraints around external rendering tools.

tasks.title: ASCII or Unicode diagram fallback renderer implement
acceptance_criteria: When rich graphics are unavailable, diagrams still render as a useful structured text fallback.
The fallback preserves directional flow and node labeling well enough for operational review.
Fallback rendering never blocks the normal document viewer.

tasks.title: Inline terminal graphics capability detection add
acceptance_criteria: The runtime can detect whether the terminal supports supported inline image protocols such as kitty, iTerm2, or sixel where implemented.
Graphic preview capability is optional and does not break non-graphic terminals.
Capability detection feeds a stable preview decision rather than trial-and-error rendering noise.

tasks.title: Read-only operator flows for goals and tasks implement
acceptance_criteria: Users can browse goals, tasks, and task details with clear selection and drilldown behavior.
Timeline and log views are available without requiring a browser fallback for common cases.
Read-only inspection never mutates task or goal state.

tasks.title: Explicit action dispatch layer implement
acceptance_criteria: Mutating commands from the TUI route through explicit client action objects or typed service calls.
There is a clear separation between inspection commands and mutation-capable commands.
The action layer can attach approval, audit, and confirmation context before dispatch.

tasks.title: Approval and confirmation UX for risky actions add
acceptance_criteria: Risky actions require explicit user confirmation with visible target scope and consequence summary.
The confirmation UX does not allow accidental key-repeat submission.
Blocked or unauthorized actions explain whether the cause is approval, policy, or auth.

tasks.title: Audit correlation from operator actions add
acceptance_criteria: TUI-originated actions carry enough metadata to correlate them in backend audit logs.
The design distinguishes view refreshes from intentional mutation requests.
Audit correlation does not require the TUI to take over audit responsibility from the hub.

tasks.title: Browser fallback bridge for unsupported deep flows add
acceptance_criteria: The operator TUI can hand off unsupported deep admin or binary-heavy flows to a browser fallback path.
Fallback targets are explicit and section-aware.
Fallback use is a conscious escape hatch rather than the default for ordinary operator workflows.

tasks.title: Session-safe cancellation and escape behavior implement
acceptance_criteria: Escape and cancel flows unwind modal state predictably without corrupting the active session.
Interrupting a long-running view refresh does not leave the UI in an inconsistent mode.
The app can close cleanly from any non-destructive state.

tasks.title: Unit tests for adapters renderers and auth bootstrap add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover profile bootstrap, local degraded-state mapping, and section adapter behavior.
Renderer tests cover markdown and diagram fallback output at minimum.
Tests isolate UI concerns from live hub dependencies where practical.

tasks.title: Interactive TUI smoke tests add
acceptance_criteria: A reproducible smoke path verifies launch, navigation, section open, refresh, and shutdown.
At least one smoke path runs against fixture transport and one against a live local hub.
Tests detect hangs or missing first paint rather than only process exit codes.

tasks.title: Performance budget and first-paint measurements add
acceptance_criteria: The operator TUI defines measurable first-paint and refresh budgets for common local-dev conditions.
Instrumentation can show which section adapters or renderers dominate startup latency.
Performance regressions can be detected before release.

tasks.title: Operator documentation and setup guide write
acceptance_criteria: Documentation explains installation, auth setup, keybindings, and fallback behavior.
The guide distinguishes the legacy shell from the real operator TUI and names the migration path.
Documentation includes terminal capability notes for markdown and diagram previews.

tasks.title: Feature flag and rollout plan define
acceptance_criteria: The new operator TUI can be introduced behind an explicit feature or command selection boundary.
Rollout steps define how local-dev, advanced users, and default users are staged.
There is a clear rollback path if the new surface regresses core operator workflows.

summary_notes: This track intentionally separates a true operator TUI from the existing legacy text-shell implementation.
The work should favor additive packaging and explicit migration boundaries over invasive replacement.
Diagram rendering is optional-rich and must always have a safe text fallback.
